data.title: Ruichang International Declares RMB 0.04 Final Dividend for 2025
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ruichang International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1334) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Ruichang International Holdings Limited has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.04 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 22 May 2026. The company plans to pay the dividend on 31 July 2026, with shares trading ex-dividend on 6 July and a record date set for 10 July, while details of the payment currency and exchange rate are yet to be announced.

The Hong Kong share register, managed by Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services, will close from 8 to 10 July 2026 to determine dividend entitlements, and no withholding tax will apply to the payout. The announcement provides income visibility for shareholders and signals management’s intention to return cash to investors, reinforcing the company’s shareholder-focused capital allocation policy.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1334) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ruichang International Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:1334 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ruichang International Holdings Ltd.

Ruichang International Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–listed equity issuer with stock code 01334. The company’s board comprises a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a standard governance structure for firms on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

data.title: Wise Living Technology Sets Final 2025 Dividend and Outlines Withholding Tax for H-Shareholders
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wise Living Technology Co., Ltd Class H ( (HK:2481) ) has shared an update.

Wise Living Technology Co., Ltd has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.17 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 15 May 2026. The record date is set for 28 May 2026, with payment scheduled for 26 June 2026, and details of the Hong Kong payment currency and exchange rate will be announced later.

The company outlined the timetable for ex-dividend trading, book closure and registration, and confirmed that it will withhold 10% tax on dividends paid to non-resident enterprise and individual H-shareholders in accordance with PRC tax regulations. Eligible investors may subsequently seek treaty benefits from tax authorities, making the announcement operationally significant for cross-border shareholders who must factor in both the payout and applicable withholding tax when assessing their post-tax returns.

More about Wise Living Technology Co., Ltd Class H

Wise Living Technology Co., Ltd is a Hong Kong-listed company whose H shares trade on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, operating within the broader technology and smart living sector. Its investor base includes both resident and non-resident enterprise and individual shareholders, with dividend distribution subject to PRC tax rules for H-shareholders registered outside mainland China.

Wise Living Technology Co., Ltd Class H ( (HK:2481) ) has issued an announcement.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ruichang International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1334) ) has issued an update.

Ruichang International Holdings reported revenue of RMB501.96 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, up from RMB444.19 million a year earlier, while gross profit rose modestly to RMB148.13 million. However, gross profit margin narrowed from 32.2% to 29.5%, and net profit fell to RMB4.62 million, with basic and diluted earnings per share declining to 1.00 RMB cent, reflecting margin pressure despite top-line growth.

The company also highlighted an adjusted non-HKFRS net profit measure, which matched reported net profit at RMB4.62 million for 2025 versus a higher adjusted figure in 2024 due to prior listing expenses. Management positions this metric as a way for investors to better assess underlying operating performance over time, though the announcement underscores that such non-standard measures have analytical limits and should not replace HKFRS-based results when evaluating the Group’s financial health.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1334) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ruichang International Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:1334 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ruichang International Holdings Ltd.

Ruichang International Holdings Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates through a group structure that generates revenue in renminbi, suggesting a primary operational base in mainland China. The company’s business model delivers products or services with relatively high gross margins around 30%, indicating a value-added focus in its chosen market segment.

data.title: China Power Proposes 2025 Final Dividend and Clarifies Tax Treatment
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has issued an announcement.

China Power International Development plans to propose a final dividend of RMB0.168 per share for the 2025 financial year, equivalent to HK$0.1911, subject to shareholder approval at its annual general meeting on 4 June 2026. The company will close its register of members at specified periods in late May and June to determine eligibility to attend and vote at the AGM and to qualify for the dividend, which is expected to be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 30 June 2026 to shareholders on record as of 12 June 2026.

The company also outlined tax treatment for the payout, confirming that a 10% PRC enterprise income tax will be withheld from the 2025 final dividend for non-resident enterprise shareholders, while natural person shareholders on the register will receive the dividend without such withholding. As China Power has been classified as a PRC tax resident enterprise by the State Taxation Administration, its PRC subsidiaries are not required to accrue withholding tax on dividends remitted to the listed company, clarifying post-tax returns and compliance obligations for different classes of investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated power company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group focuses on investment, development and operation of power generation assets in mainland China, and derives income from electricity sales and related services to a broad base of institutional and public stakeholders.

data.title: China Power’s 2025 Profit Slips as Market-Based Tariffs Pressure Renewables
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ).

China Power International Development reported a 9.6% drop in 2025 revenue to RMB49.0 billion and a 9.5% decline in net profit to RMB5.9 billion, with earnings per share down 11.1%, even as total power generation and electricity sold edged only slightly lower. Management attributed the weaker profitability mainly to reduced contributions from hydropower, wind and solar segments, despite higher revenue from wind and photovoltaic power.

The introduction of fully market-based on-grid tariffs for renewables compressed margins and, combined with lower rainfall affecting hydropower output, weighed on segment earnings. Thermal power revenue fell sharply after the partial disposal of a coal-fired subsidiary, but lower fuel costs and better coal procurement lifted thermal segment profit by about 45.8%, helping cushion the overall profit decline while the company continued expanding its clean energy capacity base.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong-incorporated power producer with a diversified portfolio spanning thermal, hydropower, wind and photovoltaic generation. The group has been shifting its asset base towards clean energy, which now accounts for more than 80% of its 54,753.7MW installed capacity, positioning it as a major player in China’s low-carbon electricity market.

Average Trading Volume: 25,001,319

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$40.82B

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ruichang International Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1334) ) has shared an update.

Ruichang International Holdings has announced the resignation of Mr. Chow Shing Lung as company secretary, authorised representative and Hong Kong process agent, effective 20 March 2026, with no disagreements reported with the board. The company has appointed Ms. Kwok Yan Ting Jennis to assume these roles, while Ms. Guo Haiyang continues as joint company secretary, and it has obtained a revised waiver from strict compliance with Listing Rules 3.28 and 8.17 following the revocation of an earlier waiver tied to Mr. Chow’s tenure, ensuring continuity in corporate governance and regulatory compliance arrangements.

The new company secretary, Ms. Kwok, brings over 12 years of corporate governance and compliance experience, along with professional governance and ESG accreditations that may strengthen the company’s sustainability oversight. Ms. Guo, who leads investment, financing, investor relations and ESG matters, retains her position as joint company secretary under the updated waiver framework, supporting the group’s strategic planning and finance functions amid tightened eligibility requirements for company secretaries under Hong Kong listing rules.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SiS International Holdings ( (HK:0529) ) has provided an update.

SiS International Holdings reported revenue of HK$9.63 billion for 2025, up from HK$9.01 billion in 2024, with gross profit rising to HK$835.4 million, supported by higher other income and reduced other losses. Despite stronger top-line performance, profit attributable to shareholders dropped to HK$60.5 million and basic earnings per share fell to 21.8 Hong Kong cents, reflecting pressure from higher income tax expense and a larger fair value loss on investment properties.

Non-controlling interests’ share of profit increased to HK$102.4 million, supporting total profit of HK$162.9 million, while total comprehensive income surged to HK$259.9 million on the back of favourable exchange differences and fair value gains on equity instruments. The results highlight a business that is expanding revenue and underlying operating income, but whose bottom line remains sensitive to property valuation movements and tax costs, with a growing share of earnings accruing to minority shareholders.

data.title: Dekon Food and Agriculture Group Grows Volumes but Sees Profit Slump in 2025
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dekon Food And Agriculture Group Class H ( (HK:2419) ) has issued an announcement.

Dekon Food and Agriculture Group reported a modest rise in operating income to RMB 23.16 billion in 2025, driven by higher pig sales volumes and largely stable poultry volumes, but the company faced a sharp decline in profitability as total profit dropped to RMB 1.42 billion from RMB 3.27 billion. Net profit attributable to equity shareholders fell to RMB 1.43 billion and earnings per share more than halved, while the board also refrained from declaring a dividend for 2025, signaling tighter returns for investors despite audited results confirming operational expansion in volume terms.

The group sold 10.83 million pigs in 2025, including more than 10.6 million to external customers and additional hogs routed through its own slaughterhouses, indicating further integration of its processing operations. Average selling prices for market hogs and poultry products declined year-on-year, particularly for hogs and yellow-feathered broilers, compressing margins and underscoring pricing pressure in China’s livestock sector even as Dekon maintained or expanded market presence through volume growth and stable production levels.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2419) stock is a Buy with a HK$73.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Dekon Food And Agriculture Group Class H stock, see the HK:2419 Stock Forecast page.

More about Dekon Food And Agriculture Group Class H

Dekon Food and Agriculture Group is a People’s Republic of China-based joint stock company focused on livestock and poultry production, primarily selling market hogs, breeding pigs, piglets and yellow-feathered broilers as well as chicks and eggs. The group serves the Chinese meat and poultry market, integrating farming and downstream slaughterhouse processing for part of its hog output.

Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2655) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Guoxia Technology Co., Ltd., a Chinese technology group listed in Hong Kong, derives its income from tech-related products and services in the mainland market. Its operations are consolidated at group level, and its financial reporting in Renminbi underlines its orientation toward China’s fast-growing technology industry.

The company reported a sharp acceleration in performance for 2025, with revenue doubling to RMB2.06 billion and profit rising 109.5% to RMB102.9 million, while gross margin improved to 18.6%. Operating cash flow surged to RMB145.2 million from RMB3.7 million, but the board maintained its conservative stance on capital returns by not recommending a final dividend, signaling a possible focus on reinvestment and balance sheet strength.

Green Energy Group Limited ( (HK:0979) ) has provided an update.

Green Energy Group Limited has agreed to sell a piece of German real estate and certain related assets in two connected transactions to entities associated with director Mr. Pek. The deals involve a property sale for EUR565,000 and an asset sale for EUR80,000, together equivalent to about HK$6.04 million.

Because the purchasers are associates of Mr. Pek, the transactions are aggregated under Hong Kong Listing Rules, making them discloseable deals with at least one percentage ratio above 5% but below 25%. The disposals, subject to specified payment conditions and German land registry procedures, represent a modest balance-sheet reshaping that triggers notification and announcement requirements but not shareholder approval.

data.title: XXF Group Delivers Revenue and Profit Growth Amid China’s Auto Boom
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XXF Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2473) ) has provided an update.

XXF Group Holdings reported solid growth for the year ended 31 December 2025, with revenue rising 27.2% year-on-year to RMB1.86 billion and gross profit up 9.3% to RMB479.1 million, while profit for the year increased 15.3% to RMB45.8 million. The modest 3.5% increase in adjusted net profit suggests margins remain under some pressure, but overall results indicate steady expansion of its auto finance and retail operations in a growing market.

The performance came against the backdrop of resilient macroeconomic conditions in China, where GDP grew 5.0% in 2025 and foreign trade continued to support economic expansion. Strong national automobile demand, supported by government policies on trade-ins, intelligent driving, new energy vehicles and low-carbon innovation, underpinned record production and sales, providing a favourable environment for XXF Group’s auto leasing and retail business and reinforcing its position in the evolving Chinese auto market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2473) stock is a Sell with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on XXF Group Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:2473 Stock Forecast page.

More about XXF Group Holdings Ltd.

XXF Group Holdings Ltd. is an established automobile retailer in China, providing automobile finance lease services primarily through self-operated sales outlets across the country. Its core businesses include non-luxury auto retail and direct finance leases, automobile operating leases and related services, as well as one-off outright car sales, positioning the group within China’s mass-market passenger vehicle segment.
